name: Vigorish Viper
description: Publicly disclosed by Infoblox in 2024. Operates a sprawling traffic distribution and AiTM infrastructure linked to illegal gambling and credential harvesting. 170K+ associated domains tracked.

killChainTemplate.explanation: Threat Defense intercepts via Infoblox NRD + Viper-cluster signature.

killChainTemplate.description: Targeted phishing to 12 finance staff with credential-harvest links.
killChainTemplate.explanation: TD blocks pre-delivery for 11 of 12 endpoints — 1 unprotected.

killChainTemplate.description: Unprotected endpoint follows link; AiTM captures Okta session token.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Endpoint protection drift — Twin flags as exposure.

killChainTemplate.description: Captured session replayed against AD federation endpoint.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Token-replay anomaly — SOC Insights opens incident.

killChainTemplate.description: Lateral movement attempt to financial database via SQLnet.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Contained at hop 2 with deployed controls.

name: VexTrio Viper
description: World's largest DNS-based Traffic Distribution System. Routes victims to scams, banking trojans, and tech-support fraud across 70K+ partner domains. Disclosed by Infoblox in early 2024.

killChainTemplate.description: Malspam delivers TDS link to payroll team.
killChainTemplate.explanation: TD blocks via Infoblox VexTrio TDS signature.
DNS Armor blocks resolution of tds-route.top across finance pool.

killChainTemplate.description: Stealer attempts to scrape Workday session cookies.
killChainTemplate.explanation: TD blocks outbound C2 — no creds reach VexTrio.

killChainTemplate.description: Stealer attempts to exfiltrate Workday session and payroll data.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Agentic Takedown filed registrar abuse report — domain seized in 2h.

name: Savvy Seahorse
description: DNS CNAME-based traffic distribution and AiTM infrastructure that aged domains for months before activation — bypassing newly-observed-domain feeds.

killChainTemplate.description: Long-aged domain matures across legitimate traffic — bypasses NRD feeds.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Domain aged beyond NRD threshold — Twin surfaces as drift.

killChainTemplate.description: Highly-tailored lure pretending to be board calendar.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Reputation feeds clean — needs SOC Insights anomaly model.

killChainTemplate.description: Session token replayed against AD federation endpoint.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Twin recommends MFA hardening on exec cluster.

killChainTemplate.description: Identity-based discovery against Vault — searching for secrets.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Twin flags lack of R&D segmentation — segment R&D from corporate AD.

name: Decoy Dog
description: Pupy-RAT-derived remote access toolkit using DNS as its primary C2 channel. Discovered by Infoblox in 2023 — operates at very low DNS-query volumes to evade detection.

killChainTemplate.description: Slow, periodic DNS queries to beacons-cdn.io.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Infoblox long-dwell DNS pattern fires — TD blocks.

killChainTemplate.description: Reused engineering credentials from prior breach data.
killChainTemplate.explanation: DNS Armor blocks resolution of Decoy Dog C2 from jump host.
Recommend Vault audit log forwarding to SOC Insights.
